title: TSRX: a Framework-Agnostic Alternative to JSX
gist: Dominic Gannaway released TSRX in alpha, a framework-agnostic TypeScript language extension positioned as a 'spiritual successor to JSX' that compiles one .tsrx file to multiple runtime targets (React, Preact, Solid, Vue, Ripple) via shared-AST plugins.

insights.claim: TSRX is a TypeScript language extension that compiles a single .tsrx component file to multiple runtime targets, with per-runtime plugins (@tsrx/react, @tsrx/preact, @tsrx/solid, @tsrx/vue, @tsrx/ripple) walking a shared AST to emit idiomatic output, so new framework targets can be added as standalone compiler plugins without changing the language.
insights.speaker: Daniel Curtis
insights.so_what: A cross-framework authoring layer could erode the lock-in advantage individual UI frameworks hold; watch whether it fragments or consolidates the JS UI tooling market.
insights.quote: TSRX compiles a single .tsrx component file to multiple runtime targets.
insights.locator: Announced on X, where Gannaway described it

insights.claim: TSRX moves common UI ergonomics from convention into syntax, adding statement-level control flow inside templates (@if, @for, @switch, @try), prop shorthands, lazy destructuring, component-scoped styles, and declarative error/async boundaries via @try/@catch/@pending instead of wrapper components.
insights.speaker: Daniel Curtis
insights.so_what: Syntax-level ergonomics signal where developer-experience competition is heading; frameworks relying on convention-based boilerplate may face pressure.
insights.quote: The headline idea is moving common UI ergonomics from convention into syntax.
insights.locator: The headline idea is moving common UI ergonomics

insights.claim: TSRX created by Dominic Gannaway, the former React and Svelte core team engineer behind Inferno, Lexical and Ripple, is open source under the MIT licence and remains in alpha, with advisers cautioning teams to 'experiment, do not migrate' and adopt per-file since a .tsrx file compiles to a .tsx equivalent.
insights.speaker: Daniel Curtis
insights.so_what: Alpha status and per-file adoption mean low immediate disruption but a credible pedigree worth tracking for medium-term ecosystem shifts.
insights.locator: That same piece cautions that the project is alpha

insights.claim: The TSRX announcement on X drew 130 replies and 1.6k likes and prompted SolidJS creator Ryan Carniato to publish an analysis, indicating notable early developer attention despite divided community sentiment.
insights.speaker: Daniel Curtis
insights.so_what: Early hype plus engagement from framework creators is a leading indicator of whether a tool gains real ecosystem momentum.
insights.locator: The announcement post on X drew 130 replies
